pubmed_links: [Nawaz et al. (2023) Biallelic variants in seven different genes associated with clinically suspected Bardet-Biedl syndrome](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37239474/);[Shamseldin et al. (2020) The morbid genome of ciliopathies: an update](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32055034/);[Brinckman et al. (2013) Cognitive, sensory, and psychosocial characteristics in patients with Bardet-Biedl syndrome](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24194441/)
summary: WDPCP is the causal gene for Bardet-Biedl syndrome type 15 (BBS15), a ciliopathy in which individuals with biallelic WDPCP variants have been documented with cognitive impairment, learning disability, and speech difficulties; autism and other neuropsychiatric comorbidities are reported as notable features of BBS more broadly. (emerging evidence)
